title: Lord mayor

infobox.value: is a title of a [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") of what is usually a major city in a [Commonwealth realm](/wiki/Commonwealth_realm "Commonwealth realm") or the former [British Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire"), with special recognition bestowed by the [sovereign](/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom "Monarchy of the United Kingdom") of the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").[[1]](#cite_note-debretts-1) [Aldermen](/wiki/Alderman "Alderman") usually elect the lord mayor from their ranks.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Municipal executive for certain large cities in the Commonwealth

**Lord mayor** is a title of a [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") of what is usually a major city in a [Commonwealth realm](/wiki/Commonwealth_realm "Commonwealth realm") or the former [British Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire"), with special recognition bestowed by the [sovereign](/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom "Monarchy of the United Kingdom") of the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").[[1]](#cite_note-debretts-1) [Aldermen](/wiki/Alderman "Alderman") usually elect the lord mayor from their ranks.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Commonwealth of Nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Commonwealth of Nations")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/89/Oxford_Lord_Mayoralty_letters_patent.jpg/250px-Oxford_Lord_Mayoralty_letters_patent.jpg)](/wiki/File:Oxford_Lord_Mayoralty_letters_patent.jpg)

Letters patent granting lord mayoralty to Oxford

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Lord_Mayor_of_London_-_John_Stuttard_-_Nov_2006.jpg/250px-Lord_Mayor_of_London_-_John_Stuttard_-_Nov_2006.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lord_Mayor_of_London_-_John_Stuttard_-_Nov_2006.jpg)

[Sir John Stuttard](/wiki/John_Stuttard "John Stuttard"), [Lord Mayor of London](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_London "Lord Mayor of London") during the 2006 [Lord Mayor's Show](/wiki/Lord_Mayor%27s_Show "Lord Mayor's Show")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1f/Arthur_Stockdale_Cope_-_Charles_Lupton.jpg/250px-Arthur_Stockdale_Cope_-_Charles_Lupton.jpg)](/wiki/File:Arthur_Stockdale_Cope_-_Charles_Lupton.jpg)

[The Right Worshipful](/wiki/The_Right_Worshipful "The Right Worshipful") Lord Mayor of Leeds [Sir Charles Lupton](/wiki/Lupton_family "Lupton family")

### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Australia")]

In [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), lord mayor is a special status granted by the [monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Australia "Monarchy of Australia") to mayors of major cities, primarily the capitals of Australian states and territories. Australian cities with lord mayors are [Adelaide](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Adelaide "Lord Mayor of Adelaide"), [Brisbane](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Brisbane "Lord Mayor of Brisbane"), [Darwin](/wiki/List_of_Mayors_and_Lord_Mayors_of_Darwin "List of Mayors and Lord Mayors of Darwin"), [Hobart](/wiki/List_of_Mayors_and_Lord_Mayors_of_Hobart "List of Mayors and Lord Mayors of Hobart"), [Melbourne](/wiki/List_of_Mayors_and_Lord_Mayors_of_Melbourne "List of Mayors and Lord Mayors of Melbourne"), [Newcastle](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Newcastle_(New_South_Wales) "Lord Mayor of Newcastle (New South Wales)"), [Parramatta](/wiki/City_of_Parramatta "City of Parramatta"), [Perth](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Perth "Lord Mayor of Perth"), [Sydney](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Sydney "Lord Mayor of Sydney"), and [Wollongong](/wiki/List_of_mayors_and_lord_mayors_of_Wollongong "List of mayors and lord mayors of Wollongong").

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Canada")]

In [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), the only town with a lord mayor in the traditional sense is [Niagara-on-the-Lake](/wiki/Niagara-on-the-Lake "Niagara-on-the-Lake"), as recognition of its role as the first capital of [Upper Canada](/wiki/Upper_Canada "Upper Canada").[[3]](#cite_note-lordy-3) Unusually, the council of [Brantford](/wiki/Brantford "Brantford"), Ontario took it upon itself to appoint an honorary Lord Mayor [Walter Gretzky](/wiki/Walter_Gretzky "Walter Gretzky") in addition to the elected mayor.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) This is the only example of a council granting the cachet itself, rather than it being granted by a higher authority, such as the Crown or national government.

### Malaysia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Malaysia")]

In the [Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language") language, the translation of lord mayor is *Datuk bandar*, which means "city chief". This title is used for the [mayor of Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Mayor_of_Kuala_Lumpur "Mayor of Kuala Lumpur"), known as *Datuk Bandar Kuala Lumpur* locally. This position is a direct appointment by the [Monarch](/wiki/King_of_Malaysia "King of Malaysia") of [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia").

### United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

Main article: [List of lord mayoralties and lord provostships in the United Kingdom](/wiki/List_of_lord_mayoralties_and_lord_provostships_in_the_United_Kingdom "List of lord mayoralties and lord provostships in the United Kingdom")

In England, Wales, and [Northern Ireland](/wiki/Northern_Ireland "Northern Ireland"), it is a purely ceremonial post conferred by [letters patent](/wiki/Letters_patent "Letters patent"). Most famously it refers to the [Lord](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_London "Lord Mayor of London") or  [Lady Mayor of London](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_London "Lord Mayor of London"), who only has jurisdiction over the [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London"), as opposed to the modern title of [Mayor of London](/wiki/Mayor_of_London "Mayor of London") governing [Greater London](/wiki/Greater_London "Greater London"). The [Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language") translation of lord mayor is *Arglwydd Faer*.

### Uganda

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Uganda")]

In [Uganda](/wiki/Uganda "Uganda"), the only jurisdiction with a [lord mayor](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Kampala "Lord Mayor of Kampala") is [Kampala](/wiki/Kampala "Kampala"), in recognition of its status as the capital city of the country.

## Ireland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Ireland")]

In [Ireland](/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland "Republic of Ireland"), the posts of [Lord Mayor of Dublin](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Dublin "Lord Mayor of Dublin") (granted under the [Kingdom of Ireland](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Ireland "Kingdom of Ireland")) and [Lord Mayor of Cork](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Cork "Lord Mayor of Cork") (granted when this city was part of the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland")) still exist, and are symbolic titles as in the UK. The Irish version of the title is *Ardmhéara*, "high mayor."

## United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: United States")]

[Annapolis](/wiki/Annapolis "Annapolis"), capital of [Province of Maryland](/wiki/Province_of_Maryland "Province of Maryland"), was the only city in the [Thirteen Colonies](/wiki/Thirteen_Colonies "Thirteen Colonies") to incorporate under a [royal charter](/wiki/Royal_charter#United_States "Royal charter"). The city used the title of [Lord Mayor of Annapolis](/wiki/Mayor_of_Annapolis "Mayor of Annapolis"), prior to the formalization of the [independence](/wiki/American_Revolution "American Revolution") of the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Equivalents in other languages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Equivalents in other languages")]

Qualified forms of mayoral titles are also present outside the [English-speaking world](/wiki/English-speaking_world "English-speaking world"), including forms such as "**high mayor**".

* In [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark"), the capital [Copenhagen](/wiki/Copenhagen "Copenhagen") has six specialised mayors dealing with specific subjects (such as schools or roads), lead by one *overborgmester*, a title commonly translated as "Lord Mayor".
* In [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), it is sometimes (and perhaps anachronistically or incongruously) used to translate German *Oberbürgermeister*, the title of the mayors of large, often county-free cities. Especially in large cities that consist of subunits governed by *District mayors* (*Bezirksbürgermeister*), the title *Oberbürgermeister* is usually used to distinguish the head executive of the entire city from those of the subunits. As in Austria, Germany's mayors serve as the actual executive leaders of their cities and are elected officials. The post of mayor in the three German [city-states](/wiki/City-state "City-state") is equivalent to that of a [Ministerpräsident](/wiki/Ministerpr%C3%A4sident "Ministerpräsident") (head of government of one of Germany's constitutive [states](/wiki/States_of_Germany "States of Germany")) and the respective post is referred to as *Regierender Bürgermeister* ("[governing mayor](/wiki/Mayor_of_Berlin "Mayor of Berlin")") in Berlin and *Erster Bürgermeister* ("[first mayor](/wiki/List_of_mayors_of_Hamburg "List of mayors of Hamburg")") in [Hamburg](/wiki/Hamburg "Hamburg"). In the third city-state, [Bremen](/wiki/Bremen_(state) "Bremen (state)"), which consists of the two cities [Bremen](/wiki/Bremen "Bremen") and [Bremerhaven](/wiki/Bremerhaven "Bremerhaven"), each city has its own mayor; the *[Bürgermeister und Präsident des Senates](/wiki/Mayor_of_Bremen "Mayor of Bremen")* (Mayor and President of the Senate) is elected by the [Bürgerschaft of Bremen](/wiki/B%C3%BCrgerschaft_of_Bremen "Bürgerschaft of Bremen") formed by elected representatives from both cities and serves as head of state government and mayor of Bremen.
* In [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), the title *Prezydent miasta* (President of the City) is used in a corresponding fashion to the German *Oberbürgermeister*, as the title of the mayors of large, county-free cities. While this title has historically been translated as "Lord Mayor" in English,[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) this usage is no longer common.
* In [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), the head city manager of the capital, [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki"), is customarily given by the country's President the title *ylipormestari* [loosely translated: "high mayor"] (which then generally is much more used of the official than *kaupunginjohtaja*, the title of the office itself), a tradition that resembles closely the lord mayoralties in other countries.
* In [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania") and [Moldova](/wiki/Moldova "Moldova"), the mayors of the capitals ([Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") and [Chişinău](/wiki/Chi%C5%9Fin%C4%83u "Chişinău"), respectively) are named Primar General which means general mayor. The name is ceremonial and it has no higher powers than mayors of other cities.
* In [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), the [mayor](/wiki/Mayor_of_Budapest "Mayor of Budapest") of the capital [Budapest](/wiki/Budapest "Budapest") is called *főpolgármester* which means chief mayor or grand mayor. Only the capital has a *főpolgármester*. Between 1873 and 1945, the [Lord Mayor of Budapest](/wiki/Lord_Mayor_of_Budapest "Lord Mayor of Budapest") was representative of the Hungarian government at the capital's municipal authority.
* In [ancient China](/wiki/History_of_China "History of China"), [jīng zhào yĭn](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E4%BA%AC%E5%85%86%E5%B0%B9 "zh:京兆尹") (京兆尹) was the title given to the mayor of capital city, jīng zhào (京兆). Today, on the other hand, city mayor and party-appointed secretary (actual leader) of the four [direct-controlled municipalities](/wiki/Direct-controlled_municipalities "Direct-controlled municipalities"), [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), [Tianjin](/wiki/Tianjin "Tianjin"), [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai"), and [Chongqing](/wiki/Chongqing "Chongqing"), though without special titles, share the rank of provincial governor and party-appointed secretary.
* In [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"), the mayor of the capital ([Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn")), was named Lord Mayor (*Ülemlinnapea*) from 1938 to 1940.
* In the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"), the mayor of the capital Prague and so-called statutory cities (listed in law, currently 25 cities) is called *Primátor*.
* In [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), the titles of mayor and lord mayor have no direct equivalent since the 1970s. The executive leader of Swedish municipalities is one of sometimes several *Kommunalråd* in the function of the chair of the municipal board. In the capital [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm") the chief executive is traditionally called *Finansborgarråd* (City Councillor of Finance)—"council" in this context referring to the executive rather than the legislative branch of local government.
* In the [Ethiopian Empire](/wiki/Ethiopian_Empire "Ethiopian Empire"), the [Mayor of Addis Ababa](/wiki/Mayor_of_Addis_Ababa "Mayor of Addis Ababa") was known as Lord Mayor ([Kantiba](/wiki/Ethiopian_aristocratic_and_court_titles "Ethiopian aristocratic and court titles")).

## Style of address

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Style of address")]

The style of address for the office of the lord mayors of Belfast, Cardiff, Bristol, the City of London, and York is [The Right Honourable](/wiki/The_Right_Honourable "The Right Honourable"). All other lord mayors are [The Right Worshipful](/wiki/The_Right_Worshipful "The Right Worshipful"). This refers only to the post, rather than the person.[[1]](#cite_note-debretts-1) The title [Sir](/wiki/Sir "Sir") can be used for [salutations](/wiki/Salutations "Salutations") when a lord mayor is being addressed.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lord_mayor&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Lord provost](/wiki/Lord_provost "Lord provost"), the similar post in [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland")
